transcript: This is a guitar, and almost everyone on earth knows what it is. In fact, over 57 million people know how to play one. But did you know that the guitar's history goes back over 4,000 years? The guitar is part of a group of instruments called chordophones. Chordophones make sound by vibrating one or more strings stretched between two points. Chordophones became popular in Europe around the 12th century, but they're really descendants of long lines of instruments like the tambour and the sitar that go back several thousands of years to ancient Central Asia and India. Most guitars are made up of six strings. Some have 12 or even more. Guitar players press the strings to the fretboard to shorten the length of the string. The shorter the string, the faster the vibration, and the higher the pitch we hear. They can play the strings one at a time, or they can strum multiple strings to create chords. The real magic happens when guitarists collaborate with singers and other instruments. They can mix and match their sounds to create endless types of music, and this has been happening since the earliest guitars were created. The guitar isn't just the staple of a good rock group, there are important parts of classical quartets and orchestras too. Guitar by Smule brings the best parts of a guitar to your mobile device, giving you the unique experience of musical collaboration between guitarist and singer. You can strum chords, emphasize particular notes, and customize your guitar's sound to play your favorite songs. You can choose the singer you'd like to play with from the Sing app by Smule, along with which new songs are released every week. So download Guitar today. It's free, and start playing the guitar right on your mobile device thanks for watching and subscribe to our channel for guitar tips tricks and tutorials every week
